content: Active agentic retrieval search agents can dynamically negotiate their own information-seeking paths via an ad-hoc agent-to-agent protocol, where each search agent proposes information needs to other domain-specialized search agents and iteratively refines its query based on reciprocal feedback, rather than following pre-programmed retrieval routes. This turns monolithic single-agent search into a distributed deliberative search process that leverages the niche knowledge of multiple agents.
source_topics: Active Agentic Retrieval with Search Agents
trigger_topic: Active Agentic Retrieval with Search Agents
